title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tyler Samaniego allowed four runs in the first inning, leading to Red Sox's 4-3 loss to the Guardians.
Brayan Bello thrived as a bulk reliever, shutting out the Guardians over seven innings with five strikeouts.
The Red Sox, now 23-33, remain in last place in the AL East after losing six of their last seven games.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Celtics fans and TD Garden's passionate environment in a recent livestream.
The Boston Celtics and Miami Heat are top contenders for Antetokounmpo, but Miami is considered the favorite.
Speculation involves a potential Jaylen Brown trade to land Antetokounmpo, potentially involving a three-team deal.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police disbanded the Harvard Senior Sunrise event on the Weeks Footbridge over crowd concerns and blocked pathways.
Massachusetts State Police responded around 5 a.m. to reports of a large crowd and attempted swimming in the Charles River.
The Harvard Class of 2026 had gathered to watch the sunrise, a tradition after their commencement ceremony.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Four Seasons' Season 2, starring Tina Fey and Will Forte, is now available on Netflix.
'The Truman Show' featuring Jim Carrey is streaming on Paramount+ and YouTube.
'Spider-Noir', starring Nicolas Cage, is available in two formats on Prime Video.

title: Red Sox predicted to draft 18-year-old third baseman in 2026 MLB Draft by ESPN
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: ESPN's Kiley McDaniel predicts Red Sox will draft Bo Lowrance, an 18-year-old third baseman, in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Bo Lowrance, a South Carolina high school player, draws comparisons to MLB's Freddie Freeman for his smooth left-handed batting.
Lowrance, a University of Virginia commit, may switch to first base due to concerns about his position at third.

title: Lowell father and daughter charged in alleged payroll tax scheme
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lim Ou, 69, and Nalen Ou, 39, from Lowell, charged with conspiracy and tax crimes related to payroll concealment.
Prosecutors allege $48 million in wages were paid in cash without reporting to the IRS between 2019 and 2025.
The Ous allegedly avoided paying over $12 million in employment taxes through their staffing agencies.

title: Report: Christian Gonzalez unlikely to participate in OTAs under current Patriots contract
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Christian Gonzalez is unlikely to participate in Patriots' OTAs due to contract extension negotiations, reports NBC Sports Boston.
Gonzalez seeks an extension for top NFL cornerback earnings; currently set to earn $4 million this season.
Patriots have cleared cap space by restructuring Mike Onwenu’s contract, creating flexibility for Gonzalez's extension.

title: Search underway for driver who led cops on a chase through Everett, Malden, Stoneham
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A suspect involved in a drug investigation led police on a chase through Everett, Malden, and Stoneham, striking a State Police cruiser.
The vehicle was abandoned on Lindenwood Road, Stoneham; the driver and a passenger fled on foot and are currently at large.
Authorities report no injuries and have stated there is no ongoing threat to public safety, urging the public to provide information.

title: Ex-Patriots HC Jerod Mayo has a new job away from football
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jerod Mayo, former Patriots head coach, became managing director of Fifth Down Capital in February 2026.
Fifth Down Capital, based in Needham, invests in technology, healthcare, and consumer companies.
Mayo, previously Optum VP, was fired after 2024 season with a 4-13 record under Patriots.

title: Mass. man reports beating Mount Everest speed climbing record
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Massachusetts native Tyler Andrews climbed Mount Everest in 9 hours and 55 minutes, surpassing the previous speed record by over an hour.
Nepali climber Lhakpa Gelu Sherpa's 2003 record of 10 hours and 56 minutes is pending verification by Nepal's mountain authorities.
Andrews dedicated his climb to Greg Krupa, supporting The Chaski Foundation's mission to aid athletes and protect mountain environments.
